title: ReadLog
description: Studio and API need readback of recent specd log lines from an **in-memory ring** populated at kernel bootstrap. v1 MUST NOT expose file-path or arbitrary filesystem log reads (security).
context: Studio and API need readback of recent specd log lines from an **in-memory ring** populated at kernel bootstrap. v1 MUST NOT expose file-path or arbitrary filesystem log reads (security).

rules.requirement: ReadLog reads only the injected ring buffer
rules: `ReadLog` MUST accept a `LogRingBuffer` at construction and `execute({ limit?, prettier? })` MUST return the newest entries (newest first) up to `limit` (default 500).
`ReadLog` MUST NOT read `specd.log` or any user-supplied path. File logging remains a separate `Logger` destination only.

When `prettier` is false, the result MUST expose `entries[]` with `timestamp`, `level`, `message`, and `context`. When `prettier` is true, the result MUST expose `lines[]` where each line is `LogFormatter.format(entry)` for the corresponding ring entry (formatter supplied at construction, default from `createLogFormatter()`).
`ReadLog` MUST accept `LogReadBuffer` and `LogFormatter` at construction. The kernel MUST pass the same `LogFormatter` instance used for `createDefaultLogger` when wiring `kernel.logs.read`.

rules.requirement: kernel exposes logs.read when ring is wired
rules: `createKernel` MUST register a callback destination that pushes to the ring when `KernelOptions.logRing` is set, and MUST attach `kernel.logs.read` as `ReadLog` for that ring.

scenarios.requirement: ReadLog reads only the injected ring buffer
scenarios.name: Newest entries returned first
given: a ring with two pushed entries
then: the first entry is the most recently pushed message

scenarios.requirement: ReadLog reads only the injected ring buffer
scenarios.name: Limit caps result size
given: a ring with more than limit entries

scenarios.name: ReadLog has no file path parameter
then: there is no files or path field
AND implementation does not call fs.readFile for log readback

scenarios.name: Prettier lines use LogFormatter
given: a formatter double that prefixes each line with fmt:
when: execute({ prettier: true }) runs on one entry with message x

scenarios.name: LogFormatter injection — primary path
when: ReadLog MUST accept LogReadBuffer and LogFormatter at construction.
then: AND no forbidden side effects occur

scenarios.name: LogFormatter injection — guard path
given: inputs that stress the requirement boundary
then: errors or skips are explicit and documented

scenarios.requirement: kernel exposes logs.read when ring is wired
scenarios.name: API server kernel has logs.read
then: AND POST log lines appear in subsequent read results
